metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4609
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 3. Superresolution microscopy of mouse hippocampal cultures reveals colocalization of SNAP25, syntaxin-1, and Munc18-1 (STXBP1) in clusters. Localizations of syntaxin-1 and SNAP25, as well as Munc18-1 and SNAP25, are correlated at nanometer scales. Note that the antibody against syntaxin-1 cross-reacts to both STX1A and STX1B.

Figure 4. In syntaxin-1A knockout mice authors observe a redundant role for the two syntaxin-1 isoforms. Significant Munc18~SNAP25 overlap remains in the syntaxin-1A-null neurons infected with control shRNA virus (A) but is lost upon syntaxin-1B shRNA knockdown (B). Superresolution localizations show that upon deletion of both syntaxin-1 isoforms, SNAP25 remains localized on the membrane whereas Munc18-1 is mostly distributed in the cytoplasm.

Taken together, syntaxin-1B (STX1B) is located in synapses at the membrane of the active zone where it forms protein complexes with SNAP25 and STXBP1. Based on the known transmembrane topology of the protein, the "integral component of ... membrane" term was chosen.. Experimental description: "Wild-Type Primary Mouse Neuronal Cultures"

"Syntaxin-1A KO Neuronal Cultures. Hippocampal neurons were cultured from neonatal syntaxin-1A KO mice"

monoclonal antibody syntaxin-1 (HPC-1; Synaptic Systems)

Regarding the syntaxin-1B shRNA knockdown;

"Development and Validation of Syntaxin-1 shRNAs. Syntaxin-1A and -1B cDNA sequences were aligned and two conserved regions were chosen as targets of shRNAs. The sequences of the two regions are AGA GGC AGC TGG AGA TCA C and GAT CAT CAT TTG CTG TGT G. The shRNA oligos were synthesized (the sense strands of the oligos were TCG ACC AGA GGC AGC TGG AGA TCA CTT CAA GA GAG TGA TCT CCA GCT GCC TCT GGT TTT TTG GAA AT, and CGC GCC CGA TCA TCA TTT GCT GT G TGT TCA AGA GAC ACA CAG CAA ATG ATG ATC ATT TTT TTG GAA A), annealed, and inserted into L309, using XhoI-XbaI and AscI and RsrII sites, respectively (6). The two shRNAs were driven by human H1 and U6 promoters, respectively. The effects of the syntaxin-1A and -1B were validated in wild-type cortical neuronal culture by Western blotting and quantitative RT-PCR. From Western blotting analysis we found that the shRNAs were more effective on syntaxin-1B than on syntaxin-1A, and thus we decided to do functional analysis on a syntaxin-1A knockout background to maximize the level of syntaxin-1 deficiency."
